title: Coarse, Medium, and Fine Styles
concept: Coarse styles control pose/shape; medium styles control facial features; fine styles control micro-details like hair texture, skin pores, and lighting.

options.text: They only let us change the overall background color.
They let us control details at different levels, like poses, facial features, and skin textures.
They force the AI to only generate cartoon characters.

exercises.explain: GANs don't just generate images randomly; they use layers to control different details from big shapes down to tiny textures!
exercises.prompt: How do style-based GANs give us precise control over the images they generate?

cards.title: The Style Control Board
cards.body: Imagine having a magical dial that lets you change a character's pose, then swap their hairstyle, and finally tweak the texture of their skin—all without messing up the other parts. That's the power of style levels!

Style-based GANs break down generation into three main levels: Coarse styles (pose, shape, gender), Medium styles (hair shape, facial features, eyes open/closed), and Fine styles (hair texture, skin pores, and lighting colors).
cards.title: Coarse, Medium, and Fine
Painting a Model Figurine
cards.body: Think of painting a miniature model figurine. First, you assemble the plastic pieces to set the pose (Coarse). Next, you paint the clothes and facial features (Medium). Finally, you add tiny highlights, dirt textures, and shading (Fine).

cards.title: Changing Pose vs. Changing Light
cards.body: If you modify only the coarse style layers, you can turn a head facing left to face right, but the hair color and skin details remain the same. If you modify only the fine style layers, the pose stays identical, but the lighting shifts from warm sun to cool studio light.

You might think changing a person's pose in a GAN requires regenerating the entire image from scratch. Actually, by changing only the coarse-style layers, the AI keeps the fine details like eye color and hair texture intact while just rotating the head!
cards.title: Start from Scratch?

right.text: Controls micro-details like skin pores, hair strands, and light reflections.

left.text: Controls overall body pose, head rotation, and face shape.

exercises.explain: Coarse styles manage the big picture like pose and shape, medium styles handle facial features and hair shapes, and fine styles handle micro-details like skin texture and lighting.

options.text: Coarse controls pose/shape, Medium controls facial features, and Fine controls textures/lighting.
Coarse controls colors, Medium controls pose, and Fine controls facial expressions.
Coarse controls lighting, Medium controls shape, and Fine controls pose.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following correctly describes the roles of Coarse, Medium, and Fine styles?
True or False: Fine styles control micro-details like hair texture, skin pores, and lighting.
exercises.explain: This is true. Fine styles operate at the highest resolution layers, affecting tiny details like skin pores, individual hair strands, and lighting.
Coarse styles handle the overall shape, structure, and pose of the subject.
exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence.
exercises.sentence: If you want to change the rotation of a generated head, you should adjust the ______ styles.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario represents an AI modifying only the FINE styles of a generated portrait?

options.text: An AI changes a face from smiling to frowning while keeping the background color identical.
An AI keeps the exact same pose and facial features, but changes the skin from smooth to showing realistic pores and wrinkles.

exercises.explain: The second scenario represents changing fine styles because the pose (coarse) and features (medium) remain identical, while only the surface texture changes.

exercises.prompt: How does a StyleGAN achieve separate control over coarse, medium, and fine details?
exercises.explain: By understanding how StyleGANs separate styles, we can mix and match them to create custom blends. Next, we will explore Style Mixing to blend features from different faces!

options.text: By training the model on lower resolution images only.
By mapping different styles to specific layers of the generator network.
By randomly deleting pixels in the final image output.
